reviews.review_description:
I’m honestly really frustrated with this game.
“Easy” difficulty does NOT feel easy at all. There are way too many enemies, and as someone who isn’t great at shooting games, it quickly becomes overwhelming and stressful instead of fun.
What annoys me the most is the save system. If I die, I restart from a checkpoint — fine. But if I quit the game, I’m thrown back to the start of the chapter, not the last checkpoint. That is incredibly frustrating and feels outdated.
The graphics are beautiful, I’ll give it that. But it doesn’t matter how good the game looks if I can’t progress because I keep dying over and over again.
I just want an actual easy mode — something more relaxed and forgiving, not this constant pressure.
The scores that rate this low are  very unfair, it’s an arcade shooter, love the cartoon graphics, gun play is fun, got it in the sale, so maybe that’s why I think I got a bargain.
Game has a serious lack of options. The gunplay is lacking both in feel and looks. The ski sections don't have joystick turning.

To devs: I like this game but from the the 3rd level or so onward, the sound of the wind makes this unplayable. Not just unpleasant but it's hurting my ears. There's a constant sound of wind that has a sound frequency that's vibrating my ear drum. I'm not even on skies or going down hill. It's a constant sound outside that keeps going even onto the next levels when you're jumping from the train. The only thing that helps is turning the SFX volume all the way down. But then I only have music and no sound effects. Please adjust a fix. I will give a 5 Star and I don't want to ask for a refund yet if this could be fixed .

reviews.review_title: This is the worst game oat
reviews.review_description: Forget anything I said about this game being good I genuinely want to kill myself after playing ts
had a great time
very basic gunplay, boring enemies and nausiating skiing

This has become my go to. One of my favorite games ever. It's so much better than haters are saying. Where else can you go skiing down a Mountain while it explodes and while you have to control your skiing and shoot the enemies and climb up a train wreck in the first 5 minutes of the game!?!? It's so bad@ss!

No thought has gone into this game at all, you don't even have a health meter. You just run around shooting everything until you find where you are actually meant to go. Such a shame because it could have been a fun game, instead it's just a bit frustrating and doesn't make me want to continue playing at all.

reviews.review_title: Great game, but the saving is rubbish
reviews.review_description: I can't play this game anymore even though I absolutely love it because the save system sends you straight back to the beginning of the level no matter how far you get in it unlike other games which save your current progress by checkpoints this has made the gaming experience  completely awful. I hope it can be remedied as i love the game
There are lots of free games with better story, graphics and game mechanics. Its worth about $5 for the 20mins of gameplay I got from it

A VR first-person shooter that was considered as one of the more popular third part titles for PSVR2, now on Meta Quest! Trying the game out in 2024 makes it feel incredibly dated. I will start by saying that if you are a new or returning VR player, you should definitely acclimate yourself to the headset before playing. There are various gameplay styles that include skiing (with head tilting as steering), climbing, shooting (duh) and puzzles. 

I can say with full confidence that when the game starts, you are not calibrated whatsoever. So you will experience one of the most nauseating experiences ever as you tumble away while stationary on your skis. Fix this by holding the Meta button down to calibrate. I wish the game ensured you were calibrated before starting. Otherwise, the levels are fairly simple with some puzzles thrown here and there, with very limited guns. The writing is atrocious but in a "corny 80s action movie" kind of vibe, and there are plenty of puns with the word "frack". 

There's some obvious political undertones about environment preservation (with a southern accent protagonist of course), but nothing really grips you enough to care about the various characters in the game. That, combined with limited enemy variation and a disappointing final boss make the game worth trying on sale at best. If the game is full price, it's a no go.

The game is very bugged, you often get stuck in the textures and there is no unstuck option - or even game saves, you need to restart from checkpoint (which doesn't even save, only full chapter does). I gave up doing the same thing multiple times because of the bugs.

Wanted to play it on PSVR when it came out but my dog(was a puppy at the time)chewed through my headset cable before I got around to it. It is a bit dated now but still holds up as a good game. I love the way you grab window seals and railing with one hand to take cover. Being able to pop out from the top or side from cover feels really cool. The special weapons are awesome though I wish they lasted a bit longer. The fact it throws in climbing parts and skiing it’s helps break up the shootouts and adds a bit more variety. The story isn’t anything to right home about but it is there so at least gives you the sense of getting something accomplished. The only thing I don’t like is it only takes around 3 hours to complete the game. I can’t complain,I bought it on sale for a little over $10. I definitely recommend this game to anyone who is okay with the 3 hour story. I did have a lot of fun in that short time

I got this game because of the run and gun action and the unique graphics and that's great and all but it's obvious that the developers didn't put any time into porting the game to meta quest, instead they just slapped it on the store and called it a day. The reason for this is on certain level it'll just glitch out and not allow you to go further like on the mountain skiing chapter where you get the the lab you walk up the the skis, but you can't even go up to them because of an invisible barrier!!! I had to restart the game fine I thought whatever it worked later in the lab chapter I got past the security door and went Into the train but it glitched me out and caused me to stay and I had to restart the entire chapter!!! That's unacceptable the developers need to actually play the game on the platform their putting it on otherwise you get all these bugs that completely ruin the experience and make me never want to play the game or support the developer.

I've been searching for another Action/Adventure/Story-Driven/FPS for the Quest 3 for quite a while and this game ticks all the boxes and then some, it is fast-paced, contains some stupendous VR mechanics, the Cell-Shaded style looks sumptuous, the combat and gun-mechanics are top notch, all of this has elevated this game very quickly into my top 5 VR games of the year so far and probably easily into my top 10 VR games ever... I had a really funny glitch during chapter four (Research and Destroy) where I inadvertently tried to hold the grenade-launcher gun in both hands, which catapulted me through the world (hilarious actually), this was quickly solved by wrestling my hands apart and I was plonked back down into the action... apart from this, the game has run faultlessly, it records natively inside the headset like a dream also and a full playthrough will commence on my Youtube Channel around the middle of October (only because I have many other daily videos already scheduled), which will hopefully encourage others to invest in this brilliant VR game... I don't want it to end, but when it does I'll be praying for a Fracked 2 ASAP please :)

developer_response.body:
Hi MaxCheesefist,

Thank you for such a detailed review! We love that you  liked the cell-shaded art style.

We're very pleased to hear that you have Fracked on your top 10 games on VR ever!

Thank you for mentioning bugs as well that we can look into. Please email support@ndreams.com for further issues :D

reviews.review_description:
I’m a little biased, because this is one of my favorite VR games of all time BUT it’s not because it’s the deepest game, or the longest but because it’s pure fast paced all out action! 

The things that make this game fun are the production, the pace and the polish. 

Yes this game is only 3-4 hours long, but it’s a game that is 100% with buying if your into fun bad*ss action games, especially at the new price of $20

The port is surprisingly good, I think the game looks fantastic and I would say the only downgrade from the PC and PlayStation versions are the quality of the explosions. They probably could’ve kept it for the Quest 3 but they didn’t go for a Quest 3 specific version, luckily the version we got is good enough that it doesn’t bother me. 

Over all, if you’re looking for the depth of a game like Saints and Sinners you have the wrong game, but if you’re looking for an awesome fast paced story driven adventure this is your game.

developer_response.body:
Hey ExcapePortal,

So glad to hear Fracked is one of your favorite VR games of all time! We're happy to know that you enjoyed the explosive and fast paced experience.

reviews.review_description:
Are we all playing the same game? 

I love the concept (fast shooting action, often while skiing) but the execution is surprisingly bland...

The hit detection is very poor and there are no signs that you've actually shot an enemy. They don't flinch from the pain of being shot and there's no blood, so you just spray and pray. 

Reloading is also strange, the new clip just floats under the gun after ejecting the spent one. This actually makes it hard to load since the controllers are already super close to each other, so there's very little room to move the clip into the gun before controllers are smacking! 

Instead of holsters, a button makes the gun magically appear or disappear from your hand. Sometimes you need to do this to climb things and sometimes you don't - the inconsistency ruins the immersion for me. 

There are ledges you cannot walk or jump off of, unless the zip line handle is still in the top position. If you've already taken it down, then you're just stuck having to walk around. 

The controls while skiing are very unpredictable and hard to master. Turn your head too fast and you'll spin out crazily! I never get sick in VR, but this game is the exception. Descent Alps handles virtual skiing way better. 

Again, I like the idea of this game. The cover mechanic of grabbing a ledge and lowering yourself is super intuitive and creative! And the fast action of shooting and skiing should be amazing, but I'm just not having fun with this one.

developer_response.body:
Hi Assassin024,

We appreciate the honest take on the game! We're glad you liked the idea of the game and found the cover mechanic intuitive and creative :D

We are sorry to hear you didn't have fun with the game and that the overall experience wasn't what you expected but we aim to continue making games that you would enjoy!

Please email support@ndreams.com for further issues.

reviews.review_description: The avalanche at the beginning of the game is bugged. My character "dies" at the same point on the slopes every time. It doesn't matter which path I take or how fast I go. It doesn't matter whether I hit anything on the way down or not. It doesn't matter what difficulty level I choose. I have watched playthroughs online and I am playing just as competently as them. Very frustrating. I've probably tried to get past this point 30 times. I am good at VR games. There is an issue with this game. I should add that there are no ski poles despite the developers showing a video with ski poles

This is better on the original PSVR, where you knew the hardware was basic and there weren't any features that were overlooked. 
Playing on quest 3 is basically a small step up from the original game graphically. 

There were many bugs after the first level where, after the first zip line, I fell into the floor and couldn't move. Because of this I had to delete and reinstall the game and once I did this haptics started working. 

The overall gameplay is the same as on the other headset, but overall this is just a completely basic port. 
If you played this before - and still have your other headset - use that and save your money. If you have a vr capable pc, buy this on there because at least the game looks better than this.

developer_response.body:
Hey Fa1coneri,

Thank you for the review and we appreciate the feedback!

We're really sorry you experienced this bug on the first level on the zipline and glad that the reinstall was able to resolve to this issue.

If you continue to experience further issues please email us on support@ndreams.com and we will do the best we can.
